You come across an ancient stone statue of Merlin the Wizard, a famous Rune Touched said to have learnt magic in the earliest ages of the world. He was an advisor to King Arthur.
You see a surprisingly large trout jump out of the river and eat another fish.

You try to pass over the river by jumping across rocks.

Success: You skillfully jump from rock to rock and reach the other side completely dry.
Failure: As you hop on a rock your foot slips and you get soaked.
You spot a fish seemingly jumping across dry land. Looking closer, you realize it's actually a frog with markings that resemble a fish, creating the illusion each time it leaps.
The wind rushes across the water, bringing with it exciting sounds and smells.
You overhear two people having a tense conversation from further upstream. 'No, he couldn't have... that's awful!'
The river rushes past some larger rocks, putting a fine mist up in the air and filling the area with the sounds of crashing water.
Two men walk passed with fishing rods strung over their shoulders, wearing widebrimmed straw hats.
Two dwarves walk passed with fishing rods strung over their shoulders, wearing widebrimmed straw hats.
Two elves walk passed with fishing rods strung over their shoulders, wearing widebrimmed straw hats.
A group of people walk passed with wicker baskets and sickles in hand, wearing widebrimmed straw hats.
You come across an old oxbow lake, partially filled with stagnant water. A frog croaks up at you from a spot in the water.
A twisted willow tree drapes branches over the edge of the water here.
A birch tree stands tall on a narrow island in the middle of the river.
The sounds of crickets, cicadas and other insects fills the air.
A passing cloud looks kinda like a fish.
A passing cloud looks kinda like a tree.
A passing cloud looks kinda like an apple.
A passing cloud looks kinda like a sword.
A passing cloud looks kinda like a pony.
A silver scaled fish leaps out of the water nearby to eat a fly before splashing back into the river.
A ladybug lands on a leaf near the edge of the river. A few seconds later a frog snaps it up with its tongue.
Oncoming clouds on the horizon make you think it'll rain later.

You come across a ramshackle campsite in a secluded clearing by the rivers edge. A curious dwarven ranger is watching you from the camps edge, whilst behind them a halfling rogue and a plasmoid wizard are cooking dinner.
You come across a meadow a short distance from the rivers edge, filled with flowers of all varieties and colours.
You spot a riverboat on the water headed slowly and steadily downstream.
You spot a riverboat on the water headed slowly and steadily upstream.
You spot a riverboat tied down with thick ropes to the riverbanks.
You spot a cute ladybug land on a leaf floating down the river.
You spot a rowboat struggling its way upstream as two figures work hard at the oars.
You come across a shallow but wide stream whick flows down over a rocky pebbled area to join the river.
You come across an area of water thick with thin green reeds for as far as the eye can see.
You spot a majestic fox come to the waters edge to drink. Surprisingly, it has two tails.
A grey coloured bird flies down to catch a fish from the river.
A frog sits on a leaf watching the river. It hops and turns around to face you, blinking as it looks at you.
You spot a family of deer on the distant edge of the river. They stand in a line bending down to sip from the river.
You spot a an elk on the distant edge of the river. It stoops its head down to sip from the waters before rising again to look across at you.
You come across deer and elk footprints in the ground near a shallow part of the river.
A fisherman awkwardly approaches you and explains that they got lost. They ask you to lead them back to town.
A lost halfling awkwardly approaches you and explains that they got lost. Could you give them directions please?

You encounter another Rune Touched on the path and recognise each other from the Base Camp. She tells you there is a bridge across the river and that there is a town at that bridge. It's located in the north.
A fisherman passing by stops to say hello. He offers over some snacks he brought with him from home.
You come across some arnica herbs, which you know can be used to make a simple healing poultice.
You come across an apple tree near the waters edge, with delicious ripe apples hanging from its branches.
You stop along your jouney to eat some travel snacks.
The river seems soo calm and peaceful here, its water beautifully reflecting the landscape around you. The natural world around you is amazing.
You come across some Riverberries which are absolutely delicious.
As you make your way along the waters a river snake takes the opportunity to leap out and bite you.
You trip and hit the ground crossing over some wet slate rocks.
A stray goblin arrow comes flying through the air to graze your arm. You're not sure where it came from.
There's a wooden walkway over a particularly swampy area of the ground near the river. Following it across, you step on a rotten board and fall to the ground below.
There sure are an awful lot of mosquitos around here.

You're about to step into a tangled piece of discarded fishing net, which you notice right at the last minute.

Success: You nimbly jump over it.
Failure: You awkwardly try to avoid tripping, but your foot gets caught in the net and you faceplant. bludgeoning damage.

You overhear singing coming from further along the river. It sounds soo beautiful...

Success: Nice music!
Failure: The song insidiously gets stuck in your head annoying you to no end, psychic damage.

You overhear singing coming from further along the river. It sounds kinda scary...

Success: Nice music!
Failure: The song fills you with dread, psychic damage.

You come across some wild herbs!

Success: You pick a few mint leaves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herb.

You come across some wild herbs!

Success: You pick a few arnica leaves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herb.

You come across some wild herbs!

Success: You pick a few water lily buds!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herbs.
Someone dropped a few coins in the water here. Finders keepers.

Almost imperceptible runes lay scattered in the water here.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A cluster of runes floats gently down the river.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

You spot a bear across the river, waiting by the edges to snap at fish. Runes gather in the area right before it swipes a minnow from the water.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Shape Water within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Animal Friendship within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Find Familiar within.
Failure: It's empty.
A large piece of driftwood has lodged upright in the mud, almost resembling a crude totem.
Half buried in the silt is a snapped wooden oar.
Fresh drag marks in the mud suggest a canoe was pulled ashore not long ago.
A family of otters slides down the bank into the water, chirping loudly.
A bright kingfisher darts low over the surface before vanishing upstream.
A strange patch of mist clings low to the water despite the warm air.
An upturned rowboat rests on the bank, paint peeling and sun-bleached.
A small lit candle drifts slowly downstream on a wooden disc.
A neat stack of river stones forms a small cairn at the water’s edge.
A broken crate has washed up here, its contents long gone.
A distant eagle cries out as its shadow passes briefly over the river.
You hear the distant roar of crashing water somewhere further along the river.
A fresh scar of earth shows where part of the bank collapsed into the river.
Hidden among thick reeds is a tiny makeshift hut barely large enough for one person.
A ring of stones and cold ash marks a recent riverside camp.
A massive log drifts lazily past, spinning slowly in the current.
In the far distance, you can just make out the silhouette of a stone bridge.
A tidy pile of fish bones rests on a flat rock by the shore.
Something large and silver flashes deep beneath the surface.
Dozens of river snails cling to a rock, leaving faint trails behind them.
A heron stands so perfectly still in the shallows it almost looks carved from stone.
The top of a wooden cart wheel protrudes from the water near the bank.
A rocky overhang casts deep shade across a quiet bend in the river.
A small whirlpool spins steadily near a cluster of submerged rocks.
A few apples bob gently along the current, bruised but intact.
A fishing net hangs tangled in a tree branch above the water.
Tiny insects skate effortlessly across the river’s surface.
You hear the plunk of a pebble striking water, but see no one nearby.
The upper half of a stone statue protrudes from the riverbed.
A wooden signpost lies broken beside the river, its lettering washed away.
A lone saddled horse drinks nervously at the river’s edge.
A swollen, waterlogged book rests open in the mud.
A sudden icy current brushes against your legs before fading.
A swarm of dragonflies hovers in a shimmering cloud above the reeds.
The remains of a burned raft are wedged between rocks.
Fresh muddy footprints lead into the river and do not return.
A heavy barge loaded with timber drifts slowly downstream.
A worn stone milestone stands crooked near the bank.
A cracked bronze handbell lies half buried in the sand.
A sturdy beaver dam spans a narrower tributary of the river.
A fox watches the water intently before slipping back into the brush.
A bleached animal skull rests atop a flat riverside stone.
Sunlight catches on a strange shimmering patch beneath the water.
The remains of a wooden dock sink slowly into the shallows.
A simple reed flute lies abandoned near the water.
A tree by the river bears initials carved deep into its bark.
A cloaked boatman poles silently past without acknowledging you.
A patch of freshly turned earth sits just above the high-water mark.
A scattering of white feathers floats along the edge of the current.
A shattered barrel leaks its last drops into the river.
An unlit lantern hangs from a branch over the water.
A thick bank of fog rolls in low along the river’s surface.
A broken spear shaft sticks upright in the muddy bank.
A painted wooden mask drifts face-up downstream.
Old stone steps descend directly into the water.
A heavy cloak lies soaked and abandoned on the shore.
A dense swarm of insects hums loudly over a still backwater.
A silver necklace glints briefly before slipping beneath the current.
From here you can see the distant ruins of a crumbling tower upriver.
Grey ash drifts across the surface from somewhere upstream.
You hear a child singing somewhere nearby, though you see no one.
A dented iron helm rests at the bottom of a shallow pool.
A wide stretch of water lilies carpets a calm section of the river.
Two crows argue loudly over something unseen in the reeds.
A rusted iron chain trails from the bank into the water.
Several villagers cheer as someone reels in a catch.
A small circle of candles and chalk symbols sits near the waterline.
All river sounds cease for a brief, unsettling moment.
A widebrimmed hat floats lazily past you.
A pair of travelers patch holes in a small boat pulled onto shore.
A flat stone skips neatly across the water from somewhere unseen.
A heavy iron anchor rests partly buried in the sand.
A faint blue glow pulses once beneath the dark water.
Two fishermen argue heatedly over a net full of fish.
A rope swing dangles from a willow branch over deep water.
A torn sail is caught high in a riverside tree.
A crude face has been carved into a boulder overlooking the river.
A rusted sword lies wedged between two stones at the water’s edge.
A low horn blast echoes faintly from somewhere downstream.
The broken remains of a wooden jetty lay sunken in the mud.
The clouds reflect perfectly in the still surface of a quiet bend of the river.
Paper lantern frames drift half-sunk along the shore.
A lone monk sits cross-legged by the water, eyes closed.
A spiral of carefully arranged pebbles decorates the sand.
A boy dives cleanly into the water from a low branch near the rivers edge.
A broken compass lies abandoned near the waterline.
A tiny shrine built of smooth stones overlooks the current.
The sun sinks low, turning the river molten gold.
A flat ferry creaks as it crosses slowly down the river.
As dusk approaches, bats begin skimming low over the water.
A sandbank sparkles faintly with flecks of mica.
Armed guards ride along the bank, scanning the water carefully.
A small coin purse lies half-buried in the muddy riverbank.
A few coins glint beneath the clear river water.
A handful of old coins and trinkets sit wedged between the river stones.
Someone must have dropped a few coins while docking here.
Scattered coins lie along the riverbank, likely dropped by a careless traveler.
Something shiny among the river pebbles turns out to be a few coins.
The current has washed a few coins onto the muddy shore.
A few coins are tangled within the reeds by the water.
A glimmer at the bottom of the shallow water catches your eye.
Coins lie scattered where travelers likely crossed the river.
A few coins sit forgotten near what may once have been a ferry stop.
Your boot nudges a small cluster of coins buried in the mud.
The remains of a rotted coin pouch lie near the riverbank.
Something flashes briefly beneath the flowing water.
A few coins appear to have fallen into the water beside an old fishing spot.
A small pile of coins sits hidden among the smooth river stones.
Coins poke out from a patch of wet sand near the water.
A few forgotten coins sit just above the waterline.
Coins lie scattered where someone once camped by the river.
The sunlight reflects off something metallic in the shallow water.

A handful of glowing runes drift slowly along the river current.

You come across something interesting...

Success: You learn something new! **Cloak of Protection** Bless a cloak with protective powers by performing a Divine Rite over 12 days, consuming a diamond worth 100 gp on each day and praying to a deity. Requires 3 CR 3 Runic Shards and a casting of the spell Warding Bond.
Failure: It's beyond you.

You come across something interesting...

Success: You learn something new! **Cloak of Protection** Enchant a cloak with protective powers by imbuing the material with arcane wards. Over 20 days inscribe 2000 gp worth of gems into sigils within the cloak and cast Mage Armor, using a CR 5 Runic Shard.
Failure: It's beyond you.

You come across something interesting...

Success: You learn something new! **Potion of Cold Resistance** Brewed over 3 days, using 1 leaf or mint, 1 leaf of Arnica and a Fish Ration along with a CR 1 Runic Shard.
Failure: It's beyond you.
